title: The One With the Princess Leia Fantasy

lines.text: Central Perk, the whole gang is entering

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm tellin' ya that girl totally winked at me.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Did not, she did not wink at you...(sees that their sacred couch is occupied by strangers).

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Huh. (They all leave, dejected)
Opening Credits
Rachel and Monica's, Phoebe, Ross, and Rachel are eating breakfast.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I have to say Tupolo Honey by Van Morrison.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nooo Way! The most romantic song ever is The Way We Were.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See, I-I think that one that Elton John wrote for, um, that guy on Who's The Boss.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What song was that, Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing) Hold me close, young Tony Dan-za.
Monica enters from her bedroom

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi Monica!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Mon!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Mon!
she just walks straight into the bathroom

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, has she slept at all?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it's been three nights in a row.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, she finally stopped crying yesterday, but then she found one of Richard's cigar butts out on the terrace, so.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay that explains it. I got a call at two in the morning, but all I could hear was, like, this high squeaky sound, so I thought okay its like a mouse or a opossum. But then I realized where would a mouse or a opossum get the money to make the phone call.
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is coming in from the bedroom

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Morning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Morning, hey, you made pancakes?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, like there's any way I could ever do that.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: (entering and singing) Monica and Rachel had syrup, now I can get my man to cheer up. (laughs hysterically) Good morning Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sarcastically) Good morning.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you know what, here's a thought. Why don't you stay home from work today and just hang out with me.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, I wish. Look, honey, you have that report to finish, and I gotta go see my lawyer.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can not believe that I am going out with someone that is getting divorced. I'm such a grown up.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: (laughs) I-I-I gotta go, I gotta go. Okay, not without a kiss.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, maybe I won't kiss you, and then you'll have to stay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (under his breath) Kiss her! Kiss her!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I'll see you later, sweetie. Bye Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: B-bye Janice. So when ya' dumpin' her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nope, not this time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on, quite yankin' me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not yanking you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is Janice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I know. She makes me happy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. All right. You look me in the eye and tell me, without blinking, that you're not breaking up with her. No blinking.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looks him in the eye) I'm not breaking up with her! (they stare at each other for a while, then Joey blows in his face)
Rachel and Monica's, Monica is entering from the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: God, look what I found in the drain.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's some of Richard's hair! (holds it close to Ross) What do I do with this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Getting it away from me would be job one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's weird, but you know what I don't wanna throw this away. I mean this is like all I have left of him, gross, drain hair. Ooh! (drops it in Ross's cereal)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh. Oh. It looks like, like a tiny little person drowning in your cereal. (Ross gives her this look, like 'Yeah, doesn't it', and gets up to dump it down the drain.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: God, what is wrong with me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You need to get some sleep.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I need to get some Richard.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, you broke up with him for a reason.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, I know. I'm just so tired of-of missing him. I'm tired of wondering why hasn't he called. Why hasn't he called!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe, because you told him not to.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you the memory woman?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Their not breaking up. Chandler and Janice. Their not breaking up. He didn't blink or anything.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you know I'm not surprised. I mean have you seen them together, they're really cute.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Cute! This is Janice! You remember Janice?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, Joey, I remember, she's annoying, but you know what she's-she's his girlfriend now. I mean what can we do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There you go! That's the spirit I'm looking for! What can we do? Huh? All right who's first? Huh? Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I'm thinking that Chandler's our friend and Janice makes him happy, so I say we just all be adult about it and accept her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, we'll call that Plan B. All right?
Ross's bedroom, Ross is working and Rachel is reading a book in bed

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (she leans over and kisses him on the cheek) Honey, I was wondering....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmm?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you still have that, um, Navy uniform?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nooo, I had to return it to the costume place.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think I have an old band uniform from high school.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You remember not having sex in high school, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well honey, what about you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean do you have any fun, you know, fantasy type things?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on you gotta have one!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, you know what...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ...if you tell me, I might do it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, umm. Did you ever see, um, Return Of The Jedi?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Do you remember the scene with, um, Jabba the Hut? Well Jabba had as, as his prisoner, um, Princess Leia.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oooh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Princess Leia, was wearing this, um, gold bikini thing. It was pretty cool.
Central Perk, Phoebe and Rachel are there

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, oh, Princess Leia and the gold bikini, every guy our age loved that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, um. It's huge. Yeah, that's the moment, when-when, you know she stopped being a princess, and became, like, a woman, you know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Did you ever do the-the Leia thing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah, um-mm. Oh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really! That-that great huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No it's just that I got this new pager and I have it on vibrate. See ya!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering with Monica in tow) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi you guys!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look who I found standing outside of the Szechwan Dragon staring at a parking meter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mon. Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why aren't you at work?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, they-they sent me home.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because I don't work at the Szechwan Dragon.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You really, really need to get some sleep, honey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know I do.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Guess what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They published my paper.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, really, let me see, let me see.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rach, look! (she holds two buns up to her ears to make her hair look like the Princess Leia 'do.) Oh, hi! Where is my strong Ross Skywalker to come rescue me. (Ross stands up horrified) There he is.
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is watching Wheel of Fortune, the puzzle is showing _oun_ Rush_ore.
[Chandler enters]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wheel!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Of!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fortune! This guy is so stupid. (yelling) It's Count Rushmore!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, you should really go on this show. All right, listen, I got three tickets to the Rangers tonight. What'd ya' say?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I say, 'I am there!' Cool! Aw, is Ross going to?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, Janice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Jan-ice. 'Cause I, just, I feel bad for Ross, you know, we-we always go together, we're like the three hocke-teers.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I may be way out on a limb here, but do you, do you, have a problem with Janice?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, Yeeees. God, how do I say this. (walks into the kitchen, Chandler follows closely, he turns around and gets startled). Oh, hi, you know that girl from the Greek restaurant with the hair (holds his hands up to signify she has big hair)?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh, that girl that I hate, eww, drives me crazy, eww, eww, oh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, I don't hate Janice, she's-she's just a lot to take, you know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, there you go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, hey. Come on man, don't look at me like that, she used to drive you nuts before too, remember?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I'm crazy about her now. I think this could be the real thing. Capital 'R'! Capital 'T'! (Joey stares at him) Don't worry, those are the right letters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, what do you want me to say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I want you to say that you like her!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't. It's like this chemical thing, you know. Every time she starts laughing, I just wanna (grimaces and tenses up) pull my arm off just so that I can have something to throw at her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks for trying. (grabs the ticket and starts to leave) Oh, and by the way there is no Count Rushmore!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, then-then who's the guy that painted the faces on the mountain? (Chandler gives him a look like 'You stupid idiot!')
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel and Ross are entering

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How could you have told her?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I didn't think it would that big of a deal.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, she didn't think it would be that big of deal.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, who are you talking to when you do that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, that was supposed to be like a private, personal thing between us.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, Ross, Phoebe is my girlfriend, okay, we tell each other everything. You know, I mean, come on, guys do the same thing, I mean, what about all that locker room stuff.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's different, okay. That's like, uh 'Who dated a stripper?' or 'Who did it on the back of the Staton Island Ferry?'.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Were both of those Joey?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Look, you don't, you don't talk about like, you know, your girlfriend and the intimate stuff you, you do with her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Not even with your best friend.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Noo!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is so sad. Your missing out on so much, Ross. I mean, the bonding and the sharing, you know. And-and knowing that someone else is going through the same thing you are.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmph. So what you, you tell each other everything?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pretty much.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did you talk about the night of five times? Do you tell people about the night of five times?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, honey, yeah that was with Carol.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know, but it's still worth mentioning, I think.
Monica's bedroom, Phoebe is trying to relax her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: ( in a soothing voice) Relax every muscle in your body. Listen to the plinky-plunky music. Okay, now close you eyes, and think of a happy place. Okay, tell me your happy place.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Richard's living room, drinking wine.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. No, no, no, not a Richard thing, just put down the glass. And get out!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry, but that's my happy place.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, okay, fine, use my happy place. Okay, I'm just gonna, I have to ask that you don't move anything.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I'll try not to.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, all right, so, your in a meadow, millions of stars in the sky....

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you think breaking up with him was a huge mistake?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, there are no questions in the happy place. Okay, just, the warm breeze, and the moonlight flowing through the trees....

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'll bet he's totally over me, I'll bet he's fine.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, betting and wagering of any kind, are, I'm sure, not permitted in the happy place. Okay. Just-just, you know, the-the lovely waterfalls, and the, the trickling fountains. And the-the calming sounds of the babbling brook....

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, this isn't working. I'm still awake and now I have to pee.
Chandler and Joey's

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: So, I hear, you hate me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I, ah, I never said hate, I was very careful about that.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: A little birdie told me something about you wanting to rip your arm off and throw it at me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And you got a 'hate' from that?! Your taking a big leap there...

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: All right, fine, fine, fine, fine, fine, we've got to do something about our little situation here Joey. So, this is my idea: you and me spending some quality time together.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But what does that gonna do...

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: For Chandler!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. I'm in.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Okay. All right. This is what we're gonna call it: 'Joey and Janice's DAY OF FUN!'

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Does it have to be a whole day?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Yes, because that's how long it takes to love me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I sleep in the next room.
Central Perk, Rachel and Monica are entering

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (crying) So, I went down to the post office, and it turns out it was those videos that I ordered for Richard about the Civil War. He loved the Civil War.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica, do you want us to take you home?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, huh. (to Ross) Or maybe to a galaxy far, far away. (Rachel, Monica, and Phoebe leave)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Women tell each other everything. Did you know that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Umm, yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No Chandler, everything! Like stuff you like, stuff she likes, technique, stamina, girth....

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Girth? Why, why, why, wh-why, why, why, why would they do this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel says sharing's great and supposedly, you know, we outta be doing it. Do you wanna?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We're not gonna talk about girth are we?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nooo!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah! All right! You go first.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay, I'll go first.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, uh, the other night Rachel and I are in bed talking about fantasies, and I happened to describe a particular Star Wars thing....

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Princess Leia in the gold bikini.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! Wow, well, that-that was easy. Okay, you-you go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, you know, you know when your in bed, with a woman.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmph.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And, ah, you know, your fooling around with her. And you get all these like, mental images in your brain, you know, like Elle MacPherson, or that girl at the Xerox place....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: With the belly-button ring? Oh, muhawa!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know, And then all of the sudden your Mom pops into your head. And your like 'Mom, get outta here!' You know, but of course, like, after that you can't possibly think of anything else, and you can't, you know, stop what your doing. So it's kinda like, you're, you know. You know...(Ross just stares at him). You don't know!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Your Mom, your telling me, your telling me, about your Mom, what is the matter with you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You said...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I said 'share' not 'scare'. Go sit over there! (Chandler goes over and sits at a table and puts his head down).
Chandler and Joey's, Joey and Janice are returning from their DAY OF FUN!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: We're baack!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you guys doing together?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Joey and Janice's DAY OF FUN!!! (laughs)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. We went to a Mets game, we got Chinese food, and you know, I love this woman. You have got competition buddy.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I just came by to give you a kiss, I have to go pick up the baby, so. I'll see you later sweetheart, you too Chandler. (laughs)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You still can't stand her can you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm sorry man, I tired, I really did.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you know, I appreciate you giving it a shot.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But, hey, look, you know the good thing is, is that we spent the whole day together and I survived, and what's even more amazing, so did she. It was bat day at Shea Stadium.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I guess that's something.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No man, that's huge! Now, I know I can stand to be around her, which means I get to hang out with you, which is kinda the whole point, anyway.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, hey, Chandler, we, ah, we stopped by the coffee shop and ran into Ross.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, if it makes you feel any better, I do it too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, I always picture your Mom when I'm having sex.
Rachel and Monica's, Monica is watching the Civil War videos

Video:April Twelve, Eighteen hundred, Sixty-One (Monica lights Richard's cigar butt), 4:30 A.M. on Tuesday, the United States garrison at Fort Sumter was fired upon (knock on door) it is now under bombardment by....
Monica answers the door

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi, Dad, what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, it's your mother's bridge night so I thought that I would come into the city for a little Monicuddle. (hugs her) Since when did you start smoking cigars?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't, I just, I just like the smell of them. So, uh, what are you really doing here Dad?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, I just wanted to make sure you were okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What makes you think that I might not be okay?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I saw Richard.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: So, how are you doing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm fine, just a little tired, I'm okay. How's Richard doing?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: You don't wanna know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I really, really do.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, he's doing terrible!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Worse than when he broke up with Barbara.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're not just saying that are you?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: No, the man is a mess.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Was he crying?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, do you think he was waiting 'til after you left, so he could cry?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Maybe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think so.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Honey, relationships are hard. Like with your Mom and me. You know after we graduated college we broke up for a while. It seems her Father, your Grandfather, wanted her to travel around Europe, like he did. Of course, he got to do it on Uncle Sam's nickel, because he was also strafing German troop trains at the time. However, (turns around and sees that Monica is sleeping and puts a blanket around her, kisses her, picks up the cigar, and starts watching the video)
Closing Credits
Ross's bedroom, Ross is humming the Star Wars theme. Rachel enters, with her hair done up like Princess Leia's, and wearing a belly dancer's outfit, to simulate the gold bikini thing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, here we go. I'm Jabba's prisoner, and you have a really weird look on your face. What? Honey, what is it? Did I get it wrong? Did I get the hair wrong? What? Did you just picture it differently? What? What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no it's, um, it's not you, um, it's um, it's (turns and sees his Mom standing where Rachel is)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Well what is it? Come on sweetie, your like, freaking me out here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I hate Chandler, the bastard ruined my life. (Rachel starts looking around and down, with a 'What the hell is going on?' look on her face.)
End
